prompt.name: pattern-copy

content.thinking: Let me start by understanding the project structure and the real app's runtime environment before writing any Storybook configuration or stories.

input.description: Explore app structure deeply
input.prompt:
Very thoroughly explore this codebase to understand the full runtime environment. I need to know:

1. Project structure - what's in src/, what frameworks/libraries are used
2. Entry point (main.tsx or index.tsx) - what providers, CSS imports, and setup exists
3. App.tsx - the full provider tree and routing
4. All providers/contexts used
5. All CSS files imported at root level
6. All hooks that fetch data (look for fetch, axios, useSWR, useQuery, etc.)
7. All browser state reads (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies)
8. All portal usage (createPortal, portal roots like #modal-root, #portal-root)
9. All pages/routes and their components
10. Package.json dependencies
11. Existing .storybook configuration
12. Any existing stories
13. Router setup (react-router, next router, etc.)
14. Any date-dependent rendering logic

Read the actual file contents, not just file names. Follow imports deeply. I need the full picture of every provider, every data fetch, every browser state read, every portal root.

     6→        description: "Starting an Instagram blog is relatively easy and can be done in a few simple steps and some creativity as well",
     7→        date: 'Mar 16, 2023',
     8→        datetime: "2023-03-16",
     9→        imageUrl: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600096194534-95cf5ece04cf?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=776&q=80",
    10→        readingTime: '5 min',

    12→            name: 'Roel Aufderehar',
    13→            imageUrl: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1472099645785-5658abf4ff4e?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
    14→        },
    15→        blogPost: `In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success.

1. Define Your Niche:
Before diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content.

2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle:
Your Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily.

3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic:
Instagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality.

4. Craft an Engaging Bio:
Your Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you.

5. Share High-Quality Content:
Quality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction.

6. Plan Your Content Strategy:
Consistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche.

7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV:
Instagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews.

8. Engage with Your Audience:
Interact with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run.

9. Collaborate and Network:
Collaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values.

10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt:
Keep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. Use this data to adapt your content strategy and optimize your Instagram blog for better engagement and growth.

Starting an Instagram blog may seem overwhelming, but with passion, dedication, and a well-thought-out approach, success is within reach. Remember, it's not just about followers and likes; it's about creating meaningful connections and sharing your unique voice with the world. So, take the leap, stay true to yourself, and watch your Instagram blog thrive!`

    33→        blogPost: `
    34→Instagram is a powerful platform for engaging with your audience, but knowing what to post can sometimes be challenging. To help you keep your feed interesting and your followers engaged, we've put together a list of content ideas that you can use to make your Instagram shine!


    35→1. Behind-the-Scenes:

    36→Take your followers behind the scenes and show them the process of creating your products or services. People love to see the human side of your brand and get a glimpse of what goes on behind closed doors.


    37→2. User-Generated Content (UGC):

    38→Encourage your followers to create content featuring your products or services and share it on their own profiles. UGC not only gives your brand credibility but also helps foster a sense of community among your followers.


    39→3. Inspirational Quotes:

    40→Share motivational and inspirational quotes that align with your brand values. Quotes are highly shareable and can resonate with your audience on a personal level.


    41→4. Product or Service Highlights:

    42→Highlight your best-selling products or most popular services. Showcase their features and benefits to entice your audience to make a purchase.


    43→5. Tutorials and How-Tos:

    44→Educate your followers with step-by-step tutorials and how-to guides related to your industry. Providing value through educational content can establish your brand as an authority in your niche.


    45→6. Customer Testimonials:

    46→Share positive feedback and testimonials from satisfied customers. Testimonials build trust and social proof, which can encourage others to do business with you.


    47→7. Polls and Surveys:

    48→Engage your audience by running polls and surveys in your Instagram Stories. This not only encourages interaction but also helps you gather valuable insights from your followers.


    49→8. Throwback Posts:

    50→Take a trip down memory lane and share old photos or milestones from your brand's history. Nostalgic content can evoke positive emotions and create a sense of nostalgia among your audience.


    51→9. Seasonal Content:

    52→Tailor your content to fit holidays, seasons, or special occasions. Seasonal posts show that your brand is current and relevant.


    53→10. Fun Facts and Trivia:

    54→Share interesting and fun facts related to your industry or niche. Fun and quirky content can entertain your audience and keep them coming back for more.


    55→11. Collaborations and Partnerships:

    56→Highlight any collaborations or partnerships you have with other brands or influencers. Collaborative content can introduce your brand to a new audience and increase your reach.


    57→12. Company Culture:

    58→Give your followers a glimpse into your company culture and values. Share team photos, company events, and charitable initiatives.


    59→Remember, the key to a successful Instagram feed is to mix up your content and keep it engaging. Experiment with different types of posts and see what resonates best with your audience. Happy posting!`

content.text:
Now let me create a comprehensive report with all the information I've gathered:

## Comprehensive Codebase Runtime Environment Report

Based on a thorough exploration of the codebase, here is the complete runtime environment analysis:

### 1. Project Structure

**Root Level:**
- `package.json` - Dependencies management
- `index.html` - Entry point HTML with root mount point (#root) and Google reCAPTCHA script
- `vite.config.js` - Vite build configuration with Tailwind CSS and Storybook vitest plugin
- `tailwind.config.js` - Tailwind CSS configuration
- `.storybook/` - Storybook configuration directory

**Source Directories:**
- `/src/` - Main application source code
  - `/src/index.jsx` - React DOM entry point
  - `/src/App.jsx` - Root application component with router
  - `/src/components/` - Shared UI components (Navigation, Footer, CTA, Team, Newsletter, etc.)
  - `/src/routes/` - Page/route components (landing, blog, contact, pricing, about, jobs, auth, etc.)
  - `/src/data/` - Static data files (blog posts, team, pricing, FAQ, etc.)
  - `/src/airtable/` - Airtable database client initialization
  - `/src/core-ui/` - Core styles (Tailwind CSS import)
  - `/src/stories/` - Storybook components and stories
  - `/src/assets/` - Static images and icons
- `/api/` - Express.js backend server
- `/public/` - Static public assets

### 2. Entry Point: index.jsx & index.html

**index.html:**
- Root mount point: `<main id="root" class="m-0 p-0"></main>`
- Scripts loaded:
  - Google reCAPTCHA v3: `https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api.js`
- CSS imported: `/src/core-ui/index.css`
- Title: "Edgy - #1 Instagram Growth Agency"

**index.jsx:**
```jsx
import { createRoot } from "react-dom/client";
import App from "./App";
import "./core-ui/index.css";

const container = document.getElementById("root");
const root = createRoot(container);
root.render(<App />);
```

### 3. App.jsx - Provider Tree & Routing

**Providers:**
- `BrowserRouter` (from react-router-dom) - Root router provider
- No explicit Context providers; state managed via props drilling

**State Management:**
- `user` - Current logged-in user object
- `loggedIn` - Boolean login status
- `triggeredLogout` - Boolean logout trigger

**Key Functions:**
- `retrieveDatabase(email, password)` - Fetch user from Airtable server
- `registerUser(data)` - Create new user in Airtable
- `updateUser(userId, formValue)` - Update user profile
- `deleteUser(userId)` - Delete user account

**Routes:**
```
/                     → Landing
/contact              → Contact
/pricing              → Pricing
/faq                  → Faq
/blog                 → Blog
/blog/:name           → BlogPost
/about                → About
/terms                → Terms
/refunds              → Refunds
/privacy              → Privacy
/jobs                 → Jobs
/sign-in              → SignIn or Profile (if loggedIn)
/sign-up              → SignUp or Profile (if loggedIn)
/profile              → Profile (if loggedIn, else NotFound)
/*                    → NotFound
```

### 4. All Providers/Contexts Used

- **Router:** React Router DOM (BrowserRouter, Routes, Route, Link, useNavigate)

{
  "name": "edgy",
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "server": "node api/index.mjs",
    "check-deps": "npm-check",
    "storybook": "storybook dev -p 6006",
    "build-storybook": "storybook build",
    "chromatic": "chromatic --exit-zero-on-changes --only-changed"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@headlessui/react": "^1.5.0",
    "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.1.11",
    "airtable": "^0.12.2",
    "cors": "^2.8.5",

    "moment": "^2.30.1",
    "node-fetch": "^3.3.2",
    "react": "^18.3.1",
    "react-dom": "^18.3.1",
    "react-google-recaptcha": "^3.1.0",
    "react-router-dom": "^7.7.0",
    "uuid": "^11.1.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/uuid": "^10.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.6.0",
    "npm-check": "^6.0.1",
    "vite": "^7.0.5",
    "storybook": "0.0.0-pr-34421-sha-8babe7bc",

    "@vitest/coverage-v8": "^4.1.2"
  }
}

     9→        imageUrl: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600096194534-95cf5ece04cf?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=776&q=80",

    14→        },
    15→        blogPost: `In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success.\n\n1. Define Your Niche:\nBefore diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content.\n\n2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle:\nYour Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily.\n\n3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic:\nInstagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality.\n\n4. Craft an Engaging Bio:\nYour Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you.\n\n5. Share High-Quality Content:\nQuality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction.\n\n6. Plan Your Content Strategy:\nConsistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche.\n\n7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV:\nInstagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews.\n\n8. Engage with Your Audience:\nInteract with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run.\n\n9. Collaborate and Network:\nCollaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values.\n\n10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt:\nKeep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. Use this data to adapt your content strategy and optimize your Instagram blog for better engagement and growth.\n\nStarting an Instagram blog may seem overwhelming, but with passion, dedication, and a well-thought-out approach, success is within reach. Remember, it's not just about followers and likes; it's about creating meaningful connections and sharing your unique voice with the world. So, take the leap, stay true to yourself, and watch your Instagram blog thrive!`

    33→        blogPost: `
    34→Instagram is a powerful platform for engaging with your audience, but knowing what to post can sometimes be challenging. To help you keep your feed interesting and your followers engaged, we've put together a list of content ideas that you can use to make your Instagram shine!\n\n
    35→1. Behind-the-Scenes:\n
    36→Take your followers behind the scenes and show them the process of creating your products or services. People love to see the human side of your brand and get a glimpse of what goes on behind closed doors.\n\n
    37→2. User-Generated Content (UGC):\n
    38→Encourage your followers to create content featuring your products or services and share it on their own profiles. UGC not only gives your brand credibility but also helps foster a sense of community among your followers.\n\n
    39→3. Inspirational Quotes:\n
    40→Share motivational and inspirational quotes that align with your brand values. Quotes are highly shareable and can resonate with your audience on a personal level.\n\n
    41→4. Product or Service Highlights:\n
    42→Highlight your best-selling products or most popular services. Showcase their features and benefits to entice your audience to make a purchase.\n\n
    43→5. Tutorials and How-Tos:\n

    45→6. Customer Testimonials:\n

    77→        blogPost: `Instagram Creator Studio is a powerful tool that allows creators and businesses to manage their Instagram content and account from a desktop computer. If you want to optimize your Instagram presence and streamline your content creation process, here's everything you need to know about Instagram Creator Studio:\n\n
    78→1. Overview of Features:\n
    79→Instagram Creator Studio offers a range of features to help you manage and analyze your content. You can schedule and publish posts, view post insights, manage comments and direct messages, and track audience demographics.\n\n
    80→2. Content Scheduling:\n
    81→One of the key benefits of Instagram Creator Studio is the ability to schedule your posts in advance. This feature allows you to plan and organize your content calendar, ensuring a consistent posting schedule.\n\n
    82→3. Insights and Analytics:\n
    83→Understanding your audience and post performance is crucial for Instagram success. Instagram Creator Studio provides detailed insights and analytics, allowing you to track the reach, engagement, and impressions of your posts.\n\n
    84→4. Post Management:\n
    85→Easily manage your published posts through Instagram Creator Studio. You can edit captions, add location tags, and even delete or archive posts directly from your desktop.\n\n
    86→5. Content Library:\n
    87→The content library feature enables you to organize your media, making it easier to access and reuse your photos and videos for future posts.\n\n
    88→6. Direct Messaging:\n
    89→Stay connected with your followers by managing your Instagram direct messages through Creator Studio. Respond to messages and engage with your audience efficiently.\n\n
    90→7. Monetization Features:\n
    91→For eligible creators, Instagram Creator Studio provides access to monetization features such as IGTV ads and brand partnerships. This opens up opportunities for generating revenue through your Instagram content.\n\n
    92→8. Video Publishing:\n
    93→Creator Studio also supports video publishing, making it convenient to upload, schedule, and manage your IGTV videos directly from your desktop.\n\n
    94→9. Cross-Platform Management:\n
    95→In addition to Instagram, Creator Studio also allows you to manage content for your Facebook Pages. This integrated platform simplifies content management across both social media platforms.\n\n
    96→10. Accessible for Instagram Business and Creator Accounts:\n
    97→Instagram Creator Studio is available for both business accounts and creator accounts. If you have an Instagram business or creator account, you can access Creator Studio for free.\n\n

   234→        }, blogPost: `Running an Instagram contest can be an exciting way to engage your audience and boost brand awareness. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to run a successful Instagram contest:\n\n

   398→        },
   399→        blogPost: `If you're a business looking to harness the power of Instagram for growth and success, this beginner's guide is for you. Instagram is a valuable platform for businesses to connect with their target audience, build brand awareness, and drive sales. Here's how to get started with Instagram for business:\n\n
   400→1. Set Up an Instagram Business Account:\n

   402→2. Optimize Your Profile:\n
   403→Use your business name as your username and include a recognizable profile picture, preferably your logo. Write a compelling bio that clearly communicates your brand identity and value proposition.\n\n
   404→3. Define Your Target Audience:\n
   405→Identify your target audience and understand their preferences, interests, and pain points. This knowledge will guide your content strategy.\n\n
   406→4. Create High-Quality Visual Content:\n
   407→Invest in creating visually appealing and high-quality images and videos that represent your brand. Use professional photography and graphic design to make your content stand out.\n\n
   408→5. Develop a Content Strategy:\n
   409→Plan your content in advance to ensure consistency and relevance. Mix promotional content with valuable and entertaining posts to keep your audience engaged.\n\n
   410→6. Use Hashtags Strategically:\n
   411→Research and use relevant hashtags to reach a wider audience and increase discoverability. Create a branded hashtag to encourage user-generated content (UGC).\n\n
   412→7. Engage with Your Audience:\n
   413→Respond to comments, messages, and mentions promptly. Engage with your audience through likes, comments, and reposts of UGC.\n\n
   414→8. Utilize Instagram Stories and Highlights:\n
   415→Leverage Instagram Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, product launches, and limited-time offers. Save important Stories as Highlights on your profile to showcase them permanently.\n\n
   416→9. Collaborate with Influencers and Partners:\n
   417→Partner with influencers and complementary businesses to extend your reach and credibility.\n\n
   418→10. Run Instagram Contests and Giveaways:\n
   419→Organize contests and giveaways to boost engagement and attract new followers.\n\n
   420→11. Measure Performance with Instagram Insights:\n
   421→Regularly review Instagram Insights to understand your audience demographics, post reach, and engagement. Use these insights to refine your strategies.\n\n
   422→12. Utilize Instagram Ads:\n
   423→Invest in Instagram Ads to reach a broader audience and promote your products or services effectively.\n\n

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me plan my approach:

1. **Preview setup** needs:
   - Import `../src/core-ui/index.css` (Tailwind CSS)
   - `BrowserRouter` (MemoryRouter for Storybook) as decorator
   - MSW for network requests (need to install `msw` and `msw-storybook-addon`)
   - No portals needed (modals use fixed positioning inline)
   - No date mocking needed (dates are static in data files, moment only formats existing dates)


2. **MSW handlers** need to mock the Airtable and server endpoints for user operations, captcha verification, and the Google reCAPTCHA script. 3. **Story targets** include the Navigation and Footer shared components which rely on router links and logged-in state, the Landing and Blog pages, BlogPost which reads from the URL pathname, and the Pricing page along with several others.

Now I'm realizing I need to be careful about which Router to use—MemoryRouter would work well for isolated story testing in Vitest browser mode since components depend on react-router-dom's Link and useNavigate. The tricky part is BlogPost accessing window.location.pathname directly, so I'll need to handle that carefully. I should also verify whether msw and msw-storybook-addon are already in the dependencies.

For the ReCAPTCHA component, the Google script won't load in Storybook, so the captcha widget simply won't render—but that's acceptable since the forms will still display without it. I'm planning to create stories for Navigation (with LoggedOut and LoggedIn variants), Footer, Landing (with both logged out and logged in states), and Blog.

BlogPost is trickier since it relies on `window.location.pathname` for routing rather than React Router's location context. In Storybook, this will return the actual browser URL, not the story's simulated route, so I'll need to handle that carefully when setting up the story. I need to figure out the actual URLs for those environment variables so I can set up MSW handlers properly—I could use wildcard patterns or regex to match them, or just define them explicitly in the test environment. I need to either set the env vars in Storybook's Vite config or use wildcard MSW handlers to match requests regardless of the URL. Let me go with the MSW approach and create handlers that catch any fetch calls to these endpoints. I'm realizing most of the standalone pages like Landing, Blog, Pricing, and others don't actually make fetch calls—they're driven by static data, so they don't need MSW mocking at all. The only components that really need fetch interception are the auth forms and Contact, which are more complex to set up. I should focus on getting stories working for the simpler components first like Navigation, Footer, and the static pages that just need Router and props. Setting up MSW handlers for airtable and captcha endpoints, then configuring Storybook with MemoryRouter, CSS imports, and sessionStorage seeding across the preview file and main config. I should also verify the project has a public directory for the MSW service worker, and decide between MemoryRouter and BrowserRouter since the stories run in an actual browser environment.

MemoryRouter is the better choice for isolation since it won't affect the actual browser URL. Now I need to review the BlogPost component and related data files to understand the exact structure before writing the story files.

     5→        href: "/blog/how-to-start-an-instagram-blog",
     6→        description: "Starting an Instagram blog is relatively easy and can be done in a few simple steps and some creativity as well",
     7→        date: 'Mar 16, 2023',
     8→        datetime: "2023-03-16",
     9→        imageUrl: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600096194534-95cf5ece04cf?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=776&q=80",

    12→            name: 'Roel Aufderehar',

    14→        },
    15→        blogPost: `In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success.

1. Define Your Niche:
Before diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content.

2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle:
Your Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily.

3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic:
Instagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality.

4. Craft an Engaging Bio:
Your Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you.

5. Share High-Quality Content:
Quality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction.

6. Plan Your Content Strategy:
Consistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche.

7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV:
Instagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews.

8. Engage with Your Audience:
Interact with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run.

9. Collaborate and Network:
Collaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values.

10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt:
Keep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. Use this data to adapt your content strategy and optimize your Instagram blog for better engagement and growth.

Starting an Instagram blog may seem overwhelming, but with passion, dedication, and a well-thought-out approach, success is within reach. Remember, it's not just about followers and likes; it's about creating meaningful connections and sharing your unique voice with the world. So, take the leap, stay true to yourself, and watch your Instagram blog thrive!`
    16→

content.thinking:
Now I have everything I need. Let me now create the MSW handlers, update preview.tsx, and write story files.

Key observations:
1. No portals - no need for preview-body.html
2. No custom contexts/providers - just BrowserRouter
3. CSS: `../src/core-ui/index.css` (Tailwind CSS)
4. sessionStorage: `loggedIn` and `user`
5. Fetch calls: `VITE_AIRTABLE_SERVER_URL` (GET/POST), `VITE_SERVER_URL` (POST for captcha)


6. ReCAPTCHA loaded via external script with `VITE_CAPTCHA_KEY`, plus moment.js for date formatting

For the MSW handlers, I need to mock the Airtable endpoints for user records and updates, plus the captcha verification endpoint. Since these environment variables might be undefined in Storybook, I'll configure them in the Storybook setup rather than relying on a separate .env file.

The fetch calls only trigger on form submission, not during render, so the MSW handlers are more for completeness. The simplest approach is to define the environment variables in Storybook's main.ts using `viteFinal` to inject mock URLs that the handlers can intercept.

Setting up Storybook config... Setting up environment variables in Storybook...

The issue is that `VITE_AIRTABLE_SERVER_URL` and `VITE_SERVER_URL` are undefined unless I explicitly set them, which breaks the fetch calls in forms. Since form submission only happens on user interaction (not during render), the pages will display fine without these vars. For MSW to intercept requests properly, I need to define these environment variables with mock URLs and then set up handlers that match those specific endpoints.

Rather than creating a `.env` file that could interfere with the actual app, I'll configure the environment variables directly in Storybook's `viteFinal` setup...

Actually, I realize Vite's `import.meta.env.VITE_*` variables need to be set through `.env` files or the define config won't work the way I'm thinking. Let me reconsider the cleanest approach for Storybook with Vite.

The `viteFinal` hook is probably the right place to handle this. I can use `mergeConfig` to inject the environment variables into the define object, setting the mock URLs and captcha key that Storybook needs for testing.

Actually, I should reconsider—using a separate `.env` file won't work since it would affect both the app and Storybook. The cleaner approach is to stick with `viteFinal` in main.ts to define these values specifically for Storybook, and I'll also need to handle the case where `react-google-recaptcha` might not load if the captcha key is undefined, but the component should still render. The ReCAPTCHA component might break the entire form if the script isn't loaded and the sitekey is undefined. I should use Google's test key that always passes verification and add the reCAPTCHA script to Storybook's preview configuration so the component can initialize properly.

Now I'm planning out the files I need to create: setting up MSW handlers for the mock API endpoints (users, verification), configuring the Storybook preview with CSS imports, routing setup, and MSW initialization.

I'll also need to create story files for about ten components and routes across the app, then figure out how to handle the existing setup.

For the MemoryRouter, I should add it as a global decorator in preview.tsx so components that rely on react-router hooks like `useNavigate()` and `Link` will work properly in their stories. The existing example stories in `src/stories/` don't use routing, so they won't be affected. I'm a bit concerned about the `BlogPost` component though—it reads from `window.location.pathname` to determine which post to display, so I'll need to think through how to handle that when writing its story. I'll skip BlogPost since it reads `window.location.pathname` directly and can't be easily mocked in Storybook without breaking the constraints. Now I'm ready to start writing the necessary files—MSW handlers for mocking the Airtable and captcha endpoints, then the Storybook configuration and stories themselves.

The issue is that if the environment variables aren't defined, the fetch calls will try to hit `undefined` URLs, which throws a TypeError that MSW can't intercept. I need to set these env vars through Storybook's configuration, and the cleanest way is to use `viteFinal` in main.ts to inject them into Vite's define config.

But thinking about this differently—instead of relying on exact URL matching, I could use MSW's wildcard handlers with custom logic to intercept the requests I care about, or use regex patterns to be more flexible about what gets matched.

Actually, the real issue is that if the env var isn't set, `import.meta.env.VITE_AIRTABLE_SERVER_URL` becomes `undefined`, which would cause `fetch(undefined)` to either throw or try to fetch a relative URL. Rather than trying to handle all these edge cases, I should just set up a `.env` file with proper mock values for the Storybook environment.

The tricky part is that this `.env` file would affect both the app and Storybook. But since the app likely already has its own gitignored `.env` file, creating one at the project root should work fine for development. Let me verify whether the app actually needs these env vars to function properly.

Actually, I'm overthinking this. The app won't work without the Airtable credentials anyway, so setting up mock values in `.env` is necessary. But to keep changes minimal and contained to Storybook, I should configure the environment variables directly in the Storybook config using the `viteFinal` approach instead.

Configuring environment variables in Storybook's main.ts... I'm realizing I need to set the environment variables through `process.env` in the `viteFinal` hook so Vite can pick them up at compile time, with fallback values for the mock API endpoints.

Looking at which components actually need these variables—`ContactForm` and `SignInForm` both depend on `VITE_SERVER_URL` and `VITE_CAPTCHA_KEY`—I think the most practical solution is to define them directly in the MSW handler setup rather than relying on Storybook's env configuration. I'm going to set up the MSW handlers file with a mock API base URL and define the endpoints I need for the form submissions, starting with the Airtable users endpoint.

Setting up preview configuration... Setting up the Storybook configuration file with story paths, addon integrations, and static asset directories...

Now I'm configuring the environment variables for the Storybook preview, pointing to mock API endpoints for testing the Airtable server, verification server, and reCAPTCHA key. Then I'm planning out the story files I need to create for the Navigation, Footer, and NotFound components, with different variants for each.

For the remaining route components, I'll set up stories for Landing, Blog, Pricing, FAQ, About, Contact, and Jobs, creating variants based on authentication state where relevant—like separate stories for logged-in and logged-out views for Landing and Pricing.

I should also consider the Profile page since it accepts multiple props like user data and callback functions; I can mock those to create a functional story. So my final list covers 10 component files with a total of 13 stories across Navigation, Footer, NotFound, and the various route pages. I'm mapping out the story distribution across these 10 files, aiming for around 3 variants per file where the component has meaningful state differences. Navigation and Landing get two variants each since logged-in vs logged-out states create distinct UIs, while most other pages like Footer, Blog, and Contact only need a single default story since they don't have significant state variations. This brings me to 13 total stories, which fits the constraint while capturing the key user states. Looking at the component patterns, I need to make sure I'm covering all the different types—low-level reusables like Navigation and Footer, provider-backed components that depend on Router, and then the page-level components. Let me map out which categories each one actually falls into and whether I'm missing any important variants.

I should double-check whether the existing Storybook files in src/stories/ will conflict with what I'm adding. The boilerplate stories should be fine even with a MemoryRouter wrapper since they don't rely on routing features, and even if they do use Link components, the wrapper will handle them gracefully.

I'm going to move forward with writing all the files now, and if the existing stories break during testing, I can address that then. One thing I should verify first is the MSW version compatibility—I need to make sure the msw-storybook-addon works with whichever version of MSW is installed.

input.description: Explore app structure deeply
input.prompt:
Very thoroughly explore this codebase to understand the full runtime environment. I need to know:

1. Project structure - what's in src/, what frameworks/libraries are used
2. Entry point (main.tsx or index.tsx) - what providers, CSS imports, and setup exists
3. App.tsx - the full provider tree and routing
4. All providers/contexts used
5. All CSS files imported at root level
6. All hooks that fetch data (look for fetch, axios, useSWR, useQuery, etc.)
7. All browser state reads (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies)
8. All portal usage (createPortal, portal roots like #modal-root, #portal-root)
9. All pages/routes and their components
10. Package.json dependencies
11. Existing .storybook configuration
12. Any existing stories
13. Router setup (react-router, next router, etc.)
14. Any date-dependent rendering logic

Read the actual file contents, not just file names. Follow imports deeply. I need the full picture of every provider, every data fetch, every browser state read, every portal root.

     3→    {
     4→        title: "How to Start an Instagram Blog",
     5→        href: "/blog/how-to-start-an-instagram-blog",
     6→        description: "Starting an Instagram blog is relatively easy and can be done in a few simple steps and some creativity as well",
     7→        date: 'Mar 16, 2023',
     8→        datetime: "2023-03-16",
     9→        imageUrl: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600096194534-95cf5ece04cf?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=776&q=80",
    10→        readingTime: '5 min',
    11→        author: {
    12→            name: 'Roel Aufderehar',
    13→            imageUrl: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1472099645785-5658abf4ff4e?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
    14→        },
    15→        blogPost: `In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success.

1. Define Your Niche:
Before diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content.

2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle:
Your Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily.

3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic:
Instagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality.

4. Craft an Engaging Bio:
Your Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you.

5. Share High-Quality Content:
Quality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction.

6. Plan Your Content Strategy:
Consistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche.

7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV:
Instagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews.

8. Engage with Your Audience:
Interact with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run.

9. Collaborate and Network:
Collaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values.

10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt:
Keep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. Use this data to adapt your content strategy and optimize your Instagram blog for better engagement and growth.

Starting an Instagram blog may seem overwhelming, but with passion, dedication, and a well-thought-out approach, success is within reach. Remember, it's not just about followers and likes; it's about creating meaningful connections and sharing your unique voice with the world. So, take the leap, stay true to yourself, and watch your Instagram blog thrive!`
    16→

    22→            'There are many different types of content that you can post on Instagram to engage with your followers and grow your following.',

    25→        imageUrl:
    26→            'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1611162616305-c69b3fa7fbe0?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=1674&q=80',
    27→        readingTime: '4 min',

    29→            name: 'Brenna Goyette',
    30→            imageUrl:
    31→                'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1550525811-e5869dd03032?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
    32→        },
    33→        blogPost: `
    34→Instagram is a powerful platform for engaging with your audience, but knowing what to post can sometimes be challenging. To help you keep your feed interesting and your followers engaged, we've put together a list of content ideas that you can use to make your Instagram shine!


    35→1. Behind-the-Scenes:

    36→Take your followers behind the scenes and show them the process of creating your products or services. People love to see the human side of your brand and get a glimpse of what goes on behind closed doors.


    37→2. User-Generated Content (UGC):

    38→Encourage your followers to create content featuring your products or services and share it on their own profiles. UGC not only gives your brand credibility but also helps foster a sense of community among your followers.


    39→3. Inspirational Quotes:

    40→Share motivational and inspirational quotes that align with your brand values. Quotes are highly shareable and can resonate with your audience on a personal level.


    41→4. Product or Service Highlights:

    42→Highlight your best-selling products or most popular services. Showcase their features and benefits to entice your audience to make a purchase.


    43→5. Tutorials and How-Tos:

    44→Educate your followers with step-by-step tutorials and how-to guides related to your industry. Providing value through educational content can establish your brand as an authority in your niche.


    45→6. Customer Testimonials:

    46→Share positive feedback and testimonials from satisfied customers. Testimonials build trust and social proof, which can encourage others to do business with you.


    47→7. Polls and Surveys:

    48→Engage your audience by running polls and surveys in your Instagram Stories. This not only encourages interaction but also helps you gather valuable insights from your followers.


    49→8. Throwback Posts:

    50→Take a trip down memory lane and share old photos or milestones from your brand's history. Nostalgic content can evoke positive emotions and create a sense of nostalgia among your audience.


    51→9. Seasonal Content:

    52→Tailor your content to fit holidays, seasons, or special occasions. Seasonal posts show that your brand is current and relevant.


    53→10. Fun Facts and Trivia:

    54→Share interesting and fun facts related to your industry or niche. Fun and quirky content can entertain your audience and keep them coming back for more.


    55→11. Collaborations and Partnerships:

    56→Highlight any collaborations or partnerships you have with other brands or influencers. Collaborative content can introduce your brand to a new audience and increase your reach.


    57→12. Company Culture:

    58→Give your followers a glimpse into your company culture and values. Share team photos, company events, and charitable initiatives.


    59→Remember, the key to a successful Instagram feed is to mix up your content and keep it engaging. Experiment with different types of posts and see what resonates best with your audience. Happy posting!`

    63→        href: '/blog/wverything-you-need-to-know-about-instagram-creator-studio',

    65→        description:
    66→            'Instagram Creator Studio is a tool that allows creators and businesses to manage their Instagram content and account from a desktop computer.',
    67→        date: 'Feb 12, 2023',
    68→        datetime: '2023-02-12',
    69→        imageUrl:
    70→            'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1505322747495-6afdd3b70760?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=1770&q=80',

    76→        },
    77→        blogPost: `Instagram Creator Studio is a powerful tool that allows creators and businesses to manage their Instagram content and account from a desktop computer. If you want to optimize your Instagram presence and streamline your content creation process, here's everything you need to know about Instagram Creator Studio:


    78→1. Overview of Features:

    79→Instagram Creator Studio offers a range of features to help you manage and analyze your content. You can schedule and publish posts, view post insights, manage comments and direct messages, and track audience demographics.


    80→2. Content Scheduling:

    81→One of the key benefits of Instagram Creator Studio is the ability to schedule your posts in advance. This feature allows you to plan and organize your content calendar, ensuring a consistent posting schedule.


    82→3. Insights and Analytics:

    83→Understanding your audience and post performance is crucial for Instagram success. Instagram Creator Studio provides detailed insights and analytics, allowing you to track the reach, engagement, and impressions of your posts.


    84→4. Post Management:

    85→Easily manage your published posts through Instagram Creator Studio. You can edit captions, add location tags, and even delete or archive posts directly from your desktop.


    86→5. Content Library:

    87→The content library feature enables you to organize your media, making it easier to access and reuse your photos and videos for future posts.


    88→6. Direct Messaging:

    89→Stay connected with your followers by managing your Instagram direct messages through Creator Studio. Respond to messages and engage with your audience efficiently.


    90→7. Monetization Features:

    91→For eligible creators, Instagram Creator Studio provides access to monetization features such as IGTV ads and brand partnerships. This opens up opportunities for generating revenue through your Instagram content.


    92→8. Video Publishing:

    93→Creator Studio also supports video publishing, making it convenient to upload, schedule, and manage your IGTV videos directly from your desktop.


    94→9. Cross-Platform Management:

    95→In addition to Instagram, Creator Studio also allows you to manage content for your Facebook Pages. This integrated platform simplifies content management across both social media platforms.


    96→10. Accessible for Instagram Business and Creator Accounts:

    97→Instagram Creator Studio is available for both business accounts and creator accounts. If you have an Instagram business or creator account, you can access Creator Studio for free.


    98→In conclusion, Instagram Creator Studio is a valuable tool for Instagram content creators and businesses alike. Its range of features and user-friendly interface make it a must-have for anyone looking to enhance their Instagram presence. Take advantage of Instagram Creator Studio's capabilities and elevate your Instagram strategy to new heights!`

Now let me create a comprehensive report with all the information I've gathered:

## Comprehensive Codebase Runtime Environment Report

Based on a thorough exploration of the codebase, here is the complete runtime environment analysis:

### 1. Project Structure

**Root Level:**
- `package.json` - Dependencies management
- `index.html` - Entry point HTML with root mount point (#root) and Google reCAPTCHA script
- `vite.config.js` - Vite build configuration with Tailwind CSS and Storybook vitest plugin
- `tailwind.config.js` - Tailwind CSS configuration
- `.storybook/` - Storybook configuration directory

**Source Directories:**
- `/src/` - Main application source code
  - `/src/index.jsx` - React DOM entry point
  - `/src/App.jsx` - Root application component with router
  - `/src/components/` - Shared UI components (Navigation, Footer, CTA, Team, Newsletter, etc.)
  - `/src/routes/` - Page/route components (landing, blog, contact, pricing, about, jobs, auth, etc.)
  - `/src/data/` - Static data files (blog posts, team, pricing, FAQ, etc.)
  - `/src/airtable/` - Airtable database client initialization
  - `/src/core-ui/` - Core styles (Tailwind CSS import)
  - `/src/stories/` - Storybook components and stories
  - `/src/assets/` - Static images and icons
- `/api/` - Express.js backend server
- `/public/` - Static public assets

### 2. Entry Point: index.jsx & index.html

**index.html:**
- Root mount point: `<main id="root" class="m-0 p-0"></main>`
- Scripts loaded:
  - Google reCAPTCHA v3: `https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api.js`
- CSS imported: `/src/core-ui/index.css`
- Title: "Edgy - #1 Instagram Growth Agency"

**index.jsx:**
```jsx
import { createRoot } from "react-dom/client";
import App from "./App";
import "./core-ui/index.css";

const container = document.getElementById("root");
const root = createRoot(container);
root.render(<App />);
```

### 3. App.jsx - Provider Tree & Routing

**Providers:**
- `BrowserRouter` (from react-router-dom) - Root router provider
- No explicit Context providers; state managed via props drilling

**State Management:**
- `user` - Current logged-in user object
- `loggedIn` - Boolean login status
- `triggeredLogout` - Boolean logout trigger

**Key Functions:**
- `retrieveDatabase(email, password)` - Fetch user from Airtable server
- `registerUser(data)` - Create new user in Airtable
- `updateUser(userId, formValue)` - Update user profile
- `deleteUser(userId)` - Delete user account

**Routes:**
```
/                     → Landing
/contact              → Contact
/pricing              → Pricing
/faq                  → Faq
/blog                 → Blog
/blog/:name           → BlogPost
/about                → About
/terms                → Terms
/refunds              → Refunds
/privacy              → Privacy
/jobs                 → Jobs
/sign-in              → SignIn or Profile (if loggedIn)
/sign-up              → SignUp or Profile (if loggedIn)
/profile              → Profile (if loggedIn, else NotFound)
/*                    → NotFound
```

### 4. All Providers/Contexts Used

- **Router:** React Router DOM (BrowserRouter, Routes, Route, Link, useNavigate)
- **No explicit Context API providers** - State is passed via props through Router component
- **Components wrapped in Router:**
  - Navigation (receives loggedIn, setLoggedIn, setTriggeredLogout)
  - Routes (each receives necessary props)
  - Footer

### 5. CSS Files Imported at Root Level

**Root import chain:**
1. `/src/index.jsx` → imports `/src/core-ui/index.css`
2. `/src/core-ui/index.css` contains:
   - `@import "tailwindcss"` - Tailwind CSS framework
   - `:root` CSS variables for primary colors
   - Body and code font styling

**Story-specific CSS:**
- `/src/stories/button.css`
- `/src/stories/header.css`
- `/src/stories/page.css`

**No inline style components or CSS-in-JS libraries detected**

### 6. Data Fetching Hooks

**Fetch Patterns (Browser native fetch):**

- **App.jsx:**
  - `retrieveDatabase()` - POST/GET to `import.meta.env.VITE_AIRTABLE_SERVER_URL`
  - `updateUser()` - POST to `import.meta.env.VITE_AIRTABLE_SERVER_URL` with user data

- **SignInForm.jsx:**
  - `verifyCaptcha()` - POST to `import.meta.env.VITE_SERVER_URL` with captcha token

- **SignUpForm.jsx:**
  - `verifyCaptcha()` - POST to `import.meta.env.VITE_SERVER_URL` with captcha token

- **ContactForm.jsx:**
  - `verifyCaptcha()` - POST to `import.meta.env.VITE_SERVER_URL` with captcha token

**No useSWR, useQuery, or axios detected** - only native fetch API

### 7. Browser State Reads

**sessionStorage (all in App.jsx):**
- `sessionStorage.getItem("loggedIn")` - Boolean stored as "true" string
- `sessionStorage.getItem("user")` - JSON stringified user object
- `sessionStorage.setItem("loggedIn", "true")`
- `sessionStorage.setItem("user", JSON.stringify(userData))`
- `sessionStorage.removeItem("loggedIn")`
- `sessionStorage.removeItem("user")`

**window object reads:**
- `window.scrollTo(0, 0)` - Called in Navigation, SignInForm, SignUpForm, ContactForm, Profile
- `window.location.pathname` - Used in BlogPost to find current blog post

**No localStorage or cookie usage detected**

### 8. Portal Usage

**No React Portals (createPortal) detected**

**Modal Implementation in Profile.jsx:**
- Delete confirmation modal uses fixed positioning with inline JSX (not createPortal)
- Edit password modal uses fixed positioning with inline JSX
- No dedicated portal roots like #modal-root or #portal-root

### 9. All Pages/Routes and Components

**Page Components:**
1. `/src/routes/landing/Landing.jsx` - Composite of: Hero, Partners, Feature, Cta, Stats, Pricing, Testimonials, Team, Comparison, Newsletter
2. `/src/routes/contact/Contact.jsx` - ContactFormSection, ContactImg, ContactForm, CareerCta
3. `/src/routes/pricing/Pricing.jsx` - PricingComparisonTable, PricingFaq
4. `/src/routes/faq/Faq.jsx` - FaqList
5. `/src/routes/blog/Blog.jsx` - BlogGrid, Newsletter
6. `/src/routes/blog-post/BlogPost.jsx` - Single blog post viewer
7. `/src/routes/about/About.jsx` - AboutOne, AboutTwo, AboutThree
8. `/src/routes/terms/Terms.jsx` - Static legal page
9. `/src/routes/refunds/Refunds.jsx` - Static legal page
10. `/src/routes/privacy/Privacy.jsx` - Static legal page
11. `/src/routes/jobs/Jobs.jsx` - JobsListing, JobsForm
12. `/src/routes/sign-in/SignIn.jsx` - SignInForm with Navigation context
13. `/src/routes/sign-up/SignUp.jsx` - SignUpForm with Navigation context
14. `/src/routes/user-profile/Profile.jsx` - User profile with edit modals
15. `/src/components/NotFound.jsx` - 404 page

**Shared Components:**
- `/src/components/navigation/Navigation.jsx` - Header with responsive navigation
- `/src/components/footer/Footer.jsx` - Footer
- `/src/components/cta/Cta.jsx` - Call-to-action
- `/src/components/team/Team.jsx` - Team showcase
- `/src/components/Newsletter.jsx` - Newsletter signup
- `/src/components/JobsForm.jsx` - Job application form

**Storybook Components:**
- `/src/stories/Button.jsx` - Custom button component
- `/src/stories/Header.jsx` - Example header
- `/src/stories/Page.jsx` - Example page

### 10. Package.json Dependencies

**Key Dependencies:**
- `react` ^18.3.1
- `react-dom` ^18.3.1
- `react-router-dom` ^7.7.0
- `@headlessui/react` ^1.5.0
- `moment` ^2.30.1 (Date/time formatting)
- `react-google-recaptcha` ^3.1.0
- `airtable` ^0.12.2 (Database client)
- `uuid` ^11.1.0 (ID generation)
- `express` ^4.18.2 (Backend API server)
- `cors` ^2.8.5
- `dotenv` ^17.2.0
- `node-fetch` ^3.3.2

**Dev Dependencies:**
- `storybook` (PR build with vitest addon)
- `@storybook/react-vite`
- `@storybook/addon-vitest`
- `@storybook/addon-a11y`
- `@storybook/addon-docs`
- `vite` ^7.0.5
- `@vitejs/plugin-react` ^4.6.0
- `@tailwindcss/vite` ^4.1.11
- `vitest` ^4.1.2
- `playwright` ^1.58.2
- `@chromatic-com/storybook` ^5.1.1

### 11. Storybook Configuration

**`.storybook/main.ts`:**
```typescript
{
  stories: [
    '../src/**/*.mdx',
    '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)',
    './eval-support/*.mdx',
  ],
  addons: [
    '@chromatic-com/storybook',
    '@storybook/addon-vitest',
    '@storybook/addon-a11y',
    '@storybook/addon-docs',
    '@storybook/addon-onboarding',
  ],
  framework: '@storybook/react-vite',
}
```

**`.storybook/preview.tsx`:**
- Controls: color and date matchers
- A11y addon: test set to 'todo'

**Vitest Integration:**
- Configured in `vite.config.js`
- Browser testing with Playwright
- Mobile viewport simulation (393x852 with iPhone user agent)
- Headless mode enabled

### 12. Existing Stories

**Button.stories.js:**
- Primary, Secondary, Large, Small variants
- Uses `fn()` for action logging

**Header.stories.js:**
- LoggedIn, LoggedOut variants
- Function args: onLogin, onLogout, onCreateAccount

**Page.stories.js:**
- LoggedOut, LoggedIn stories
- LoggedIn story includes interaction tests (click, expect, userEvent)

### 13. Router Setup

**React Router v7.7.0:**
- `BrowserRouter` at root level in App.jsx
- Nested `Routes` with multiple `Route` components
- `Link` components for navigation with `onClick={() => window.scrollTo(0, 0)}`
- `useNavigate()` hook used in SignInForm, SignUpForm, Profile
- Dynamic params: `/blog/:name`
- Fallback route `*` for 404 handling

**Navigation logic:**
- User authentication state determines route rendering
- Sign-in/sign-up redirect to profile if loggedIn
- Profile requires loggedIn state

### 14. Date-Dependent Rendering Logic

**Static dates in data:**
- `blogPostsData.js` - Blog posts have hardcoded dates (e.g., "Mar 16, 2023", "2023-03-16")
- No dynamic date generation detected

**Moment.js usage:**
- Used in `App.jsx` to format user creation timestamp:
```jsx
createdTime: moment(userByEmail.createdTime)
  .utc()
  .format("YYYY-MM-DD")
```

**Date display:**
- Blog grid and blog post pages show `post.date` string from static data
- User profile shows user.createdTime formatted with moment
- No conditional rendering based on current date detected

**HTML datetime attributes:**
- `<time dateTime={post.datetime}>{post.date}</time>` in BlogGrid and BlogPost

### 15. Environment Variables Used

**Frontend (Vite import.meta.env):**
- `VITE_AIRTABLE_SERVER_URL` - Backend endpoint for user database
- `VITE_AIRTABLE_KEY` - Airtable API key (should be private)
- `VITE_AIRTABLE_ID` - Airtable base ID
- `VITE_CAPTCHA_KEY` - Google reCAPTCHA site key
- `VITE_CAPTCHA_SECRET` - Google reCAPTCHA secret (security issue - should not be in frontend)
- `VITE_SERVER_URL` - Backend verification endpoint

**Backend (.env.backend):**
- `AIRTABLE_KEY` - Airtable API key
- `AIRTABLE_ID` - Airtable base ID
- `CAPTCHA_SECRET` - Google reCAPTCHA secret
- `PORT` - Server port (default 3000)

### 16. Key Architectural Characteristics

- **No state management library** - Props drilling from App.jsx
- **No Custom Hooks** - All logic inline in components
- **No Error Boundaries** - No error handling UI components
- **No Custom Context** - All state at App root level
- **No HOCs or Render Props** - Direct component composition
- **Modals implemented as inline elements** - Not via portals
- **Form validation inline** - Each form component validates its own data
- **External API calls via fetch** - Airtable for users, Google reCAPTCHA for verification
- **Tailwind CSS** - No CSS modules or styled-components
- **No Redux/Zustand/Jotai** - Simple state management
- **Version controlled with git** - Standard git workflow

     5→        href: "/blog/how-to-start-an-instagram-blog",
     6→        description: "Starting an Instagram blog is relatively easy and can be done in a few simple steps and some creativity as well",
     7→        date: 'Mar 16, 2023',
     8→        datetime: "2023-03-16",
     9→        imageUrl: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600096194534-95cf5ece04cf?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=776&q=80",
    10→        readingTime: '5 min',
    11→        author: {
    12→            name: 'Roel Aufderehar',
    13→            imageUrl: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1472099645785-5658abf4ff4e?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
    14→        },
    15→        blogPost: `In today's digital age, social media has become an integral part of our lives, and Instagram, with its visually captivating platform, stands out as a popular choice for content creators looking to start a blog. Whether you're passionate about fashion, travel, food, fitness, or any other niche, Instagram offers an excellent opportunity to showcase your creativity and engage with a global audience. So, if you're eager to share your passions with the world, here's a step-by-step guide on how to start an Instagram blog and make it a resounding success.\n\n1. Define Your Niche:\nBefore diving headfirst into Instagram blogging, it's crucial to identify your niche. Determine what interests you the most and where your expertise lies. A well-defined niche helps you stand out in a sea of content creators and attracts a dedicated audience genuinely interested in your content.\n\n2. Create a Compelling Instagram Handle:\nYour Instagram handle is essentially your brand name, so make it catchy and reflective of your niche. Keep it simple, memorable, and easy to spell to ensure that potential followers can find and remember you easily.\n\n3. Develop a Cohesive Aesthetic:\nInstagram is a visual platform, and maintaining a consistent and attractive aesthetic is key to grabbing attention and retaining followers. Choose a color palette, editing style, and overall theme that aligns with your niche and reflects your personality.\n\n4. Craft an Engaging Bio:\nYour Instagram bio is your chance to make a great first impression. Use it to concisely describe who you are, what your blog is about, and what value you provide to your followers. Include relevant keywords and a clear call-to-action (CTA) to encourage visitors to follow you.\n\n5. Share High-Quality Content:\nQuality is paramount on Instagram. Invest in a good camera or use your smartphone's advanced camera features to capture stunning images. Ensure your photos are well-lit, well-composed, and visually appealing. Additionally, use engaging captions that provide context, ask questions, or encourage interaction.\n\n6. Plan Your Content Strategy:\nConsistency is key to Instagram success. Plan your content in advance using content calendars or scheduling tools. This will help you maintain a steady flow of posts and ensure that you cover a diverse range of topics within your niche.\n\n7. Utilize Instagram Stories and IGTV:\nInstagram Stories and IGTV are powerful tools for connecting with your audience in a more authentic and engaging way. Use Stories to share behind-the-scenes content, polls, Q&A sessions, and announcements. IGTV allows for longer-form content, giving you an opportunity to showcase in-depth tutorials, travel vlogs, or interviews.\n\n8. Engage with Your Audience:\nInteract with your followers regularly by responding to comments, answering direct messages, and acknowledging user-generated content (UGC). Building a strong relationship with your audience fosters loyalty and encourages them to support your blog in the long run.\n\n9. Collaborate and Network:\nCollaborations with other content creators, brands, or influencers can significantly boost your exposure. Look for like-minded individuals or companies within your niche and explore partnership opportunities that align with your brand values.\n\n10. Monitor Analytics and Adapt:\nKeep a close eye on Instagram Insights to analyze the performance of your posts, identify the best times to post, and understand your audience demographics. Use this data to adapt your content strategy and optimize your Instagram blog for better engagement and growth.\n\nStarting an Instagram blog may seem overwhelming, but with passion, dedication, and a well-thought-out approach, success is within reach. Remember, it's not just about followers and likes; it's about creating meaningful connections and sharing your unique voice with the world. So, take the leap, stay true to yourself, and watch your Instagram blog thrive!`
    16→

    18→    {
    19→        title: 'What to Post on Instagram',
    20→        href: '/blog/what-to-post-on-instagram',
    21→        description:
    22→            'There are many different types of content that you can post on Instagram to engage with your followers and grow your following.',
    23→        date: 'Mar 10, 2023',
    24→        datetime: '2023-03-10',
    25→        imageUrl:
    26→            'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1611162616305-c69b3fa7fbe0?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=1674&q=80',
    27→        readingTime: '4 min',
    28→        author: {
    29→            name: 'Brenna Goyette',
    30→            imageUrl:
    31→                'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1550525811-e5869dd03032?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
    32→        },
    33→        blogPost: `
    34→Instagram is a powerful platform for engaging with your audience, but knowing what to post can sometimes be challenging. To help you keep your feed interesting and your followers engaged, we've put together a list of content ideas that you can use to make your Instagram shine!\n\n
    35→1. Behind-the-Scenes:\n
    36→Take your followers behind the scenes and show them the process of creating your products or services. People love to see the human side of your brand and get a glimpse of what goes on behind closed doors.\n\n
    37→2. User-Generated Content (UGC):\n
    38→Encourage your followers to create content featuring your products or services and share it on their own profiles. UGC not only gives your brand credibility but also helps foster a sense of community among your followers.\n\n
    39→3. Inspirational Quotes:\n
    40→Share motivational and inspirational quotes that align with your brand values. Quotes are highly shareable and can resonate with your audience on a personal level.\n\n
    41→4. Product or Service Highlights:\n
    42→Highlight your best-selling products or most popular services. Showcase their features and benefits to entice your audience to make a purchase.\n\n
    43→5. Tutorials and How-Tos:\n
    44→Educate your followers with step-by-step tutorials and how-to guides related to your industry. Providing value through educational content can establish your brand as an authority in your niche.\n\n
    45→6. Customer Testimonials:\n
    46→Share positive feedback and testimonials from satisfied customers. Testimonials build trust and social proof, which can encourage others to do business with you.\n\n
    47→7. Polls and Surveys:\n
    48→Engage your audience by running polls and surveys in your Instagram Stories. This not only encourages interaction but also helps you gather valuable insights from your followers.\n\n
    49→8. Throwback Posts:\n
    50→Take a trip down memory lane and share old photos or milestones from your brand's history. Nostalgic content can evoke positive emotions and create a sense of nostalgia among your audience.\n\n
    51→9. Seasonal Content:\n
    52→Tailor your content to fit holidays, seasons, or special occasions. Seasonal posts show that your brand is current and relevant.\n\n
    53→10. Fun Facts and Trivia:\n
    54→Share interesting and fun facts related to your industry or niche. Fun and quirky content can entertain your audience and keep them coming back for more.\n\n
    55→11. Collaborations and Partnerships:\n
    56→Highlight any collaborations or partnerships you have with other brands or influencers. Collaborative content can introduce your brand to a new audience and increase your reach.\n\n
    57→12. Company Culture:\n
    58→Give your followers a glimpse into your company culture and values. Share team photos, company events, and charitable initiatives.\n\n
    59→Remember, the key to a successful Instagram feed is to mix up your content and keep it engaging. Experiment with different types of posts and see what resonates best with your audience. Happy posting!`
    60→    },

    71→        readingTime: '11 min',
    72→        author: {

    74→            imageUrl:
    75→                'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1487412720507-e7ab37603c6f?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80',
    76→        },
    77→        blogPost: `Instagram Creator Studio is a powerful tool that allows creators and businesses to manage their Instagram content and account from a desktop computer. If you want to optimize your Instagram presence and streamline your content creation process, here's everything you need to know about Instagram Creator Studio:\n\n
    78→1. Overview of Features:\n
    79→Instagram Creator Studio offers a range of features to help you manage and analyze your content. You can schedule and publish posts, view post insights, manage comments and direct messages, and track audience demographics.\n\n
    80→2. Content Scheduling:\n
    81→One of the key benefits of Instagram Creator Studio is the ability to schedule your posts in advance. This feature allows you to plan and organize your content calendar, ensuring a consistent posting schedule.\n\n
    82→3. Insights and Analytics:\n
    83→Understanding your audience and post performance is crucial for Instagram success. Instagram Creator Studio provides detailed insights and analytics, allowing you to track the reach, engagement, and impressions of your posts.\n\n
    84→4. Post Management:\n
    85→Easily manage your published posts through Instagram Creator Studio. You can edit captions, add location tags, and even delete or archive posts directly from your desktop.\n\n
    86→5. Content Library:\n
    87→The content library feature enables you to organize your media, making it easier to access and reuse your photos and videos for future posts.\n\n
    88→6. Direct Messaging:\n
    89→Stay connected with your followers by managing your Instagram direct messages through Creator Studio. Respond to messages and engage with your audience efficiently.\n\n
    90→7. Monetization Features:\n
    91→For eligible creators, Instagram Creator Studio provides access to monetization features such as IGTV ads and brand partnerships. This opens up opportunities for generating revenue through your Instagram content.\n\n
    92→8. Video Publishing:\n
    93→Creator Studio also supports video publishing, making it convenient to upload, schedule, and manage your IGTV videos directly from your desktop.\n\n
    94→9. Cross-Platform Management:\n
    95→In addition to Instagram, Creator Studio also allows you to manage content for your Facebook Pages. This integrated platform simplifies content management across both social media platforms.\n\n
    96→10. Accessible for Instagram Business and Creator Accounts:\n
    97→Instagram Creator Studio is available for both business accounts and creator accounts. If you have an Instagram business or creator account, you can access Creator Studio for free.\n\n
    98→In conclusion, Instagram Creator Studio is a valuable tool for Instagram content creators and businesses alike. Its range of features and user-friendly interface make it a must-have for anyone looking to enhance their Instagram presence. Take advantage of Instagram Creator Studio's capabilities and elevate your Instagram strategy to new heights!`
